So what we're going to do is we're going to take some questions. You've got some questions for Dan about referrals. Now is the time to ask. Alright. Okay, so the first question is, what are specific rewards that a company should give customers for referring other customers? The best. The best that the value of the customer will permit. So this really starts with math, not with creativity. So the math questions are, what is it cost you to get a customer called off the street? Again, most people don't know, but they need to know. They need to at least estimate. They need to get to a, what we call maximum allowable number. What is it cost you to get a customer? What will you spend to get a customer off the street? What's the most that you will spend? That now gives us the maximum that you should be cheerfully willing to spend to get a referral. So the same amount that you would spend. Why not? You're getting a better customer. Right. Well, why don't you? You should actually be willing to spend more, technically.
